content:
# Well Design

**Summary**: The planning and construction of a well to maximize production.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014]] used well-design to optimize the placement and length of horizontal wells.

- [[2014-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 2014]] evaluated different well-designs, specifically U-shaped vs. conventional horizontal wells.

- [[2019-elzenary|Elzenary, 2019]] considered well geometry as a factor in hole cleaning difficulty.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] designed geopolymer systems based on specific wellbore requirements for HPHT zones.

## Scientific Context

- [[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014]] Used to determine the best location for horizontal wells.

- [[2014-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 2014]] evaluated different well-designs, specifically U-shaped vs. conventional horizontal wells.

- [[2019-elzenary|Elzenary, 2019]] considered well geometry as a factor in hole cleaning difficulty.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] designed geopolymer systems based on specific wellbore requirements for HPHT zones.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014]] Simulation modeling.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Optimizing vertical placement and length of reservoir contact can improve effectiveness. ([[2014-al-julaih|Al-Julaih, 2014, p. 16]])


- U-shaped design improves flow efficiency by reducing the impact of frictional pressure losses. ([[2014-al-gahtani|Al-Gahtani, 2014, p. 130]])

- Hole cleaning is most difficult at an inclination of approximately 60 degrees. ([[2019-elzenary|Elzenary, 2019, p. 32]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]])

- Slurries were designed for 150-194°F BHCT and 200-300°F BHST to meet deep well needs. ([[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023, p. 22]])
